originalTitle: Wall Street rallies, Dow ends with record on US-Iran deal
summary: STORY: Wall Street's main indexes rallied on Monday, with the Dow gaining nearly 1% to notch a record-high close, the S&P 500 climbing about one-and-two-thirds percent and the Nasdaq soaring 3%. Investors cheered the news of a preliminary deal between the U.S. and Iran to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Kevin Mahn is president and chief investment officer at Hennion & Walsh Asset Management. "I believe that the theme of the day for investors is that a deal has been reached. We think the strait has been opened, we think, and the market has rallied, at least for now. Obviously, if the first two tenets of that theme start to get unwound, then we're going to see this market rally go in the opposite direction. But as it stands now, we believe that ultimately a deal between the U.S. and Iran will be signed in Switzerland on Friday and in all likelihood, tankers will start to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, and that should start to ease inflationary pressures that are a result of higher oil prices that have been in place now since the beginning of March." U.S. crude futures settled down 4.9% and hit their lowest level since March, helping to lift shares of energy-sensitive airline and cruise companies and hurting energy stocks. Easing inflation fears also helped boost technology stocks as investors were more comfortable taking on riskier bets. Chip giant Nvidia gained 3.5% and Micron Technology jumped nearly 11% after at least two brokerages sharply raised their price targets for the stock. Investors now turn their attention to the Federal Reserve's two-day policy meeting, which concludes Wednesday. Traders expect the central bank to leave interest rates unchanged, but they'll be watching closely for signals from Kevin Warsh in his first press conference as Fed chair.

chineseTitle: 华尔街反弹，道指收盘，美伊协议创历史新高
chineseSummary: 故事：华尔街主要股指周一上涨，道琼斯指数上涨近 1%，创下历史收盘新高，标准普尔 500 指数上涨约三分之二，纳斯达克指数飙升 3%。美国和伊朗达成初步协议以结束战争并重新开放霍尔木兹海峡的消息令投资者欢呼雀跃。 Kevin Mahn 是 Hennion & Walsh 资产管理公司的总裁兼首席投资官。 “我认为投资者今天的主题是达成协议。我们认为海峡已经开放，市场已经反弹，至少目前是这样。显然，如果该主题的前两个原则开始放松，那么我们将看到市场反弹朝相反的方向发展。但就目前情况而言，我们相信美国和伊朗之间的协议最终将在周五在瑞士签署，油轮很可能将启动通过霍尔木兹海峡，这应该会开始缓解自三月初以来油价上涨造成的通胀压力。”美国原油期货下跌 4.9%，触及 3 月份以来的最低水平，帮助提振了对能源敏感的航空公司和游轮公司的股价，但损害了能源股。通胀担忧的缓解也有助于提振科技股，因为投资者更愿意承担风险较高的赌注。至少两家券商大幅提高了该股的目标价后，芯片巨头英伟达股价上涨 3.5%，美光科技股价上涨近 11%。投资者现在将注意力转向周三结束的美联储为期两天的政策会议。交易员预计央行将维持利率不变，但他们将密切关注凯文·沃什(Kevin Warsh)在其首次新闻发布会上发出的信号。

whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
